root_text.an5.139:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato rañño nāgo na rājāraho hoti na rājabhoggo, na rañño aṅgaṁtveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an5.139:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ, akkhamo saddānaṁ, akkhamo gandhānaṁ, akkhamo rasānaṁ, akkhamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:2.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato hatthikāyaṁ vā disvā assakāyaṁ vā disvā rathakāyaṁ vā disvā pattikāyaṁ vā disvā saṁsīdati visīdati, na santhambhati na sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:2.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:3.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti saddānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato hatthisaddaṁ vā sutvā assasaddaṁ vā sutvā rathasaddaṁ vā sutvā pattisaddaṁ vā sutvā bheripaṇavasaṅkhatiṇavaninnādasaddaṁ vā sutvā saṁsīdati visīdati, na santhambhati na sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:3.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti saddānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:4.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti gandhānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato ye te rañño nāgā abhijātā saṅgāmāvacarā tesaṁ muttakarīsassa gandhaṁ ghāyitvā saṁsīdati visīdati, na santhambhati na sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:4.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti gandhānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:5.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti rasānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato ekissā vā tiṇodakadattiyā vimānito dvīhi vā tīhi vā catūhi vā pañcahi vā tiṇodakadattīhi vimānito saṁsīdati visīdati, na santhambhati na sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:5.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti rasānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:6.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:6.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato ekena vā saravegena viddho, dvīhi vā tīhi vā catūhi vā pañcahi vā saravegehi viddho saṁsīdati visīdati, na santhambhati na sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:6.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo akkhamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:7.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi aṅgehi samannāgato rañño nāgo na rājāraho hoti na rājabhoggo na rañño aṅgaṁtveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an5.139:8.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi aṅgehi samannāgato bhikkhu na āhuneyyo hoti na pāhuneyyo na dakkhiṇeyyo na añjalikaraṇīyo na anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an5.139:8.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ, akkhamo saddānaṁ, akkhamo gandhānaṁ, akkhamo rasānaṁ, akkhamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:9.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:9.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā rajanīye rūpe sārajjati, na sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:9.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti rūpānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:10.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti saddānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:10.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sotena saddaṁ sutvā rajanīye sadde sārajjati, na sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:10.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti saddānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:11.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti gandhānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:11.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ghānena gandhaṁ ghāyitvā rajanīye gandhe sārajjati, na sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:11.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti gandhānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:12.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti rasānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:12.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā rajanīye rase sārajjati, na sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:12.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti rasānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:13.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:13.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāyena phoṭṭhabbaṁ phusitvā rajanīye phoṭṭhabbe sārajjati, na sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:13.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu akkhamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:14.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu na āhuneyyo hoti na pāhuneyyo na dakkhiṇeyyo na añjalikaraṇīyo na anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an5.139:15.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, aṅgehi samannāgato rañño nāgo rājāraho hoti rājabhoggo, rañño aṅgaṁtveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an5.139:15.3: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti rūpānaṁ, khamo saddānaṁ, khamo gandhānaṁ, khamo rasānaṁ, khamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:16.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti rūpānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:16.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato hatthikāyaṁ vā disvā assakāyaṁ vā disvā rathakāyaṁ vā disvā pattikāyaṁ vā disvā na saṁsīdati na visīdati, santhambhati sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:16.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti rūpānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:17.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti saddānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:17.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato hatthisaddaṁ vā sutvā assasaddaṁ vā sutvā rathasaddaṁ vā sutvā pattisaddaṁ vā sutvā bheripaṇavasaṅkhatiṇavaninnādasaddaṁ vā sutvā na saṁsīdati na visīdati, santhambhati sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:17.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti saddānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:18.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti gandhānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:18.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato ye te rañño nāgā abhijātā saṅgāmāvacarā tesaṁ muttakarīsassa gandhaṁ ghāyitvā na saṁsīdati na visīdati, santhambhati sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:18.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti gandhānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:19.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti rasānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:19.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato ekissā vā tiṇodakadattiyā vimānito dvīhi vā tīhi vā catūhi vā pañcahi vā tiṇodakadattīhi vimānito na saṁsīdati na visīdati, santhambhati sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:19.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti rasānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:20.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:20.2: Idha, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo saṅgāmagato ekena vā saravegena viddho, dvīhi vā tīhi vā catūhi vā pañcahi vā saravegehi viddho na saṁsīdati na visīdati, santhambhati sakkoti saṅgāmaṁ otarituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:20.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, rañño nāgo khamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:21.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi aṅgehi samannāgato rañño nāgo rājāraho hoti rājabhoggo, rañño aṅgaṁtveva saṅkhaṁ gacchati.
root_text.an5.139:22.1: Evamevaṁ kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an5.139:22.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti rūpānaṁ, khamo saddānaṁ, khamo gandhānaṁ, khamo rasānaṁ, khamo phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:23.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti rūpānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:23.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā rajanīye rūpe na sārajjati, sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:23.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti rūpānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:24.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti saddānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:24.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sotena saddaṁ sutvā rajanīye sadde na sārajjati, sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:24.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti saddānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:25.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti gandhānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:25.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ghānena gandhaṁ ghāyitvā rajanīye gandhe na sārajjati, sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:25.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti gandhānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:26.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti rasānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:26.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu jivhāya rasaṁ sāyitvā rajanīye rase na sārajjati, sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:26.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti rasānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:27.1: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ?
root_text.an5.139:27.2: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kāyena phoṭṭhabbaṁ phusitvā rajanīye phoṭṭhabbe na sārajjati, sakkoti cittaṁ samādahituṁ.
root_text.an5.139:27.3: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu khamo hoti phoṭṭhabbānaṁ.
root_text.an5.139:28.1: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.

translation_text.an5.139:1.1: “Mendicants, a royal bull elephant with five factors is not worthy of a king, not fit to serve a king, and is not reckoned a factor of kingship.
translation_text.an5.139:1.3: It’s when a royal bull elephant can’t endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.139:2.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can’t endure sights?
translation_text.an5.139:2.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle falters and founders at the sight of a division of elephants, of cavalry, of chariots, or of infantry. It doesn’t stay firm, and fails to plunge into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:2.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can’t endure sights.
translation_text.an5.139:3.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can’t endure sounds?
translation_text.an5.139:3.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle falters and founders at the sound of a division of elephants, of cavalry, of chariots, or of infantry, or the thunder of the drums, kettledrums, horns, and cymbals. It doesn’t stay firm, and fails to plunge into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:3.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can’t endure sounds.
translation_text.an5.139:4.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can’t endure smells?
translation_text.an5.139:4.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle falters and founders when it smells the odor of the feces and urine of battle-hardened, pedigree royal bull elephants. It doesn’t stay firm, and fails to plunge into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:4.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can’t endure smells.
translation_text.an5.139:5.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can’t endure tastes?
translation_text.an5.139:5.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle falters and founders when it misses a meal of grass and water, or it misses two, three, four, or five meals. It doesn’t stay firm, and fails to plunge into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:5.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can’t endure tastes.
translation_text.an5.139:6.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can’t endure touches?
translation_text.an5.139:6.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle falters and founders when struck by a swift arrow, or by two, three, four, or five swift arrows. It doesn’t stay firm, and fails to plunge into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:6.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can’t endure touches.
translation_text.an5.139:7.1: A royal bull elephant with these five factors is not worthy of a king, not fit to serve a king, and is not reckoned a factor of kingship.
translation_text.an5.139:8.1: In the same way, a mendicant with five qualities is not worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, not worthy of hospitality, not worthy of a religious donation, not worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is not the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an5.139:8.3: It’s when a mendicant can’t endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.139:9.1: And how is it that a mendicant can’t endure sights?
translation_text.an5.139:9.2: It’s when a mendicant, seeing a sight with their eyes, is aroused by a desirable sight, so is not able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:9.3: That’s how a mendicant can’t endure sights.
translation_text.an5.139:10.1: And how is it that a mendicant can’t endure sounds?
translation_text.an5.139:10.2: It’s when a mendicant, hearing a sound with their ears, is aroused by a desirable sound, so is not able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:10.3: That’s how a mendicant can’t endure sounds.
translation_text.an5.139:11.1: And how is it that a mendicant can’t endure smells?
translation_text.an5.139:11.2: It’s when a mendicant, smelling an odor with their nose, is aroused by a desirable smell, so is not able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:11.3: That’s how a mendicant can’t endure smells.
translation_text.an5.139:12.1: And how is it that a mendicant can’t endure tastes?
translation_text.an5.139:12.2: It’s when a mendicant, tasting a flavor with their tongue, is aroused by desirable tastes, so is not able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:12.3: That’s how a mendicant can’t endure tastes.
translation_text.an5.139:13.1: And how is it that a mendicant can’t endure touches?
translation_text.an5.139:13.2: It’s when a mendicant, feeling a touch with their body, is aroused by a desirable touch, so is not able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:13.3: That’s how a mendicant can’t endure touches.
translation_text.an5.139:14.1: A mendicant with these five qualities is not worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, not worthy of hospitality, not worthy of a religious donation, not worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is not the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an5.139:15.1: A royal bull elephant with five factors is worthy of a king, fit to serve a king, and is reckoned a factor of kingship.
translation_text.an5.139:15.3: It’s when a royal bull elephant can endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.139:16.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can endure sights?
translation_text.an5.139:16.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle does not falter or founder at the sight of a division of elephants, of cavalry, of chariots, or of infantry. It stays firm, and plunges into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:16.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can endure sights.
translation_text.an5.139:17.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can endure sounds?
translation_text.an5.139:17.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant does not falter or founder at the sound of a division of elephants, of cavalry, of chariots, or of infantry, or the thunder of the drums, kettledrums, horns, and cymbals. It stays firm, and plunges into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:17.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can endure sounds.
translation_text.an5.139:18.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can endure smells?
translation_text.an5.139:18.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle does not falter or founder when it smells the odor of the feces and urine of battle-hardened, pedigree royal bull elephants. It stays firm, and plunges into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:18.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can endure smells.
translation_text.an5.139:19.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can endure tastes?
translation_text.an5.139:19.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle does not falter or founder when it misses a meal of grass and water, or it misses two, three, four, or five meals. It stays firm, and plunges into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:19.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can endure tastes.
translation_text.an5.139:20.1: And how is it that a royal bull elephant can endure touches?
translation_text.an5.139:20.2: It’s when a royal bull elephant gone to battle does not falter or founder when struck by a swift arrow, or by two, three, four, or five swift arrows. It stays firm, and plunges into battle.
translation_text.an5.139:20.3: That’s how a royal bull elephant can endure touches.
translation_text.an5.139:21.1: A royal bull elephant with these five factors is worthy of a king, fit to serve a king, and is reckoned a factor of kingship.
translation_text.an5.139:22.1: In the same way, a mendicant with five qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an5.139:22.3: It’s when a mendicant can endure sights, sounds, smells, tastes, and touches.
translation_text.an5.139:23.1: And how is it that a mendicant can endure sights?
translation_text.an5.139:23.2: It’s when a mendicant, seeing a sight with their eyes, is not aroused by a desirable sight, so is able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:23.3: That’s how a mendicant can endure sights.
translation_text.an5.139:24.1: And how is it that a mendicant can endure sounds?
translation_text.an5.139:24.2: It’s when a mendicant, hearing a sound with their ears, is not aroused by desirable sounds, so is able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:24.3: That’s how a mendicant can endure sounds.
translation_text.an5.139:25.1: And how is it that a mendicant can endure smells?
translation_text.an5.139:25.2: It’s when a mendicant, smelling an odor with their nose, is not aroused by a desirable smell, so is able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:25.3: That’s how a mendicant can endure smells.
translation_text.an5.139:26.1: And how is it that a mendicant can endure tastes?
translation_text.an5.139:26.2: It’s when a mendicant, tasting a flavor with their tongue, is not aroused by desirable tastes, so is able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:26.3: That’s how a mendicant can endure tastes.
translation_text.an5.139:27.1: And how is it that a mendicant can endure touches?
translation_text.an5.139:27.2: It’s when a mendicant, feeling a touch with their body, is not aroused by a desirable touch, so is able to still the mind.
translation_text.an5.139:27.3: That’s how a mendicant can endure touches.
translation_text.an5.139:28.1: A mendicant with these five qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.”

variant_text.an5.139:5.2: vimānito → vihanīto (sya-all); vihānito (katthaci) | vimānito → vihanīto (sya-all); vihānito (katthaci)
